en.value: Nepal aims to graduate from a Least Developed Country status to a middle-income country by 2026. However, low productivity and high unemployment rates have led to large labour out migration and subsequent heavy reliance on remittances. Digital transformation represents an opportunity for a structural transformation of the economy. In Nepal, a rising pool of digital skills and tech start-ups, combined with the growth of small and medium enterprises (SME) in the tech sector, presents an avenue for improving overall economic efficiency and broadening Nepal's export-basket. However, SMEs in the tech sector face challenges, i.e., poor access to finance (debt and equity) and business development services. They represent a negligible portion of Nepali bank and financial institution’s loan portfolio as banks have limited knowledge of this client segment.Nepal Impact Investment Community 2 (NIIC2) is a digital economy focused fund which will facilitate debt and equity investment in digital economy catalysts SMEs in Nepal by attracting investors, i.e. development finance institutions, assisting banks to adapt their loan products to the needs of SMEs in the tech sector, and providing pre- and post-loan business development services to these SMEs. However, the capital requirement for high growth DEC SMEs is such that only debt investment does not suffice and needs to be complemented with equity. Therefore, NIIC2 through its equity fund will enable access to long term patient capital, in the form of equity, for high growth potential DEC SMEs.Digital Economy Catalysts (DEC) SMEs, led by increased private investment, expand market and create jobs and income opportunities for women and men in Nepal, including from discriminated groups.
